metadata.title: MPs' Financial Interests, Gifts & Expenses
metadata.description: Data from the Register of Members' Financial Interests (Parliament) and the Independent Parliamentary Standards Authority (IPSA). The Register tracks declared outside earnings, gifts, hospitality, shareholdings, and other interests. IPSA publishes all parliamentary expenses claims.

primarySources.name: Register of Members' Financial Interests
primarySources.notes: Updated every 2 weeks. 10 categories of declarable interest. Thresholds: external employment >£100 per payment, gifts/hospitality >£300, donations >£1,500. Published under Open Parliament Licence.

primarySources.name: Independent Parliamentary Standards Authority (IPSA)
primarySources.notes: Publishes all MP business costs and expenses every 2 months. Individual claims searchable. Annual publications since 2010-11.

metadata.methodologyNote: Interests data compiled from Parliamentary Commissioner for Standards publications and verified journalism citing the official register. Expenses data from IPSA annual publications. Historical top-earner data (2019-2024) is from the cumulative register. Current Parliament data covers July 2024 onward.

expenses.description: Total MP business costs by financial year, from IPSA annual publications
expenses.methodologyNote: Covers staffing, office, accommodation, and travel costs. Staffing typically accounts for 76-80% of total. Individual claims data available from May 2010. Aggregate totals below from IPSA annual publications and House of Commons Library analysis.

annualTotals.notes: IPSA confirmed. Travel fell sharply due to COVID-19.
Estimated from IPSA 2023 publication using category ratios.
Estimated from IPSA Nov 2024 'Supporting Democracy' report.

categoryNotes.staffing: MP staff salaries and employer NICs. Consistently 76-80% of total costs.
categoryNotes.accommodation: London or constituency accommodation for MPs with constituencies outside London.
categoryNotes.travel: Rail, mileage, air travel between constituency and Westminster.

topEarners.description: MPs with highest declared external income, current Parliament (since July 2024)
topEarners.sourceName: Register of Members' Financial Interests / openDemocracy analysis
topEarners.methodologyNote: Figures from declared registrable interests since the July 2024 General Election. Includes employment, broadcasting, consultancy, rental income, and speaking fees. Does not include ministerial salary.

currentParliament.name: Nigel Farage
currentParliament.notes: £415k from Direct Bullion for 12 hours' work (£11,250/hr). 17 code of conduct breaches found by Parliamentary Commissioner.

historicalTopEarners.name: Boris Johnson
historicalTopEarners.notes: Cumulative total as MP for Uxbridge.

aggregateStats.description: Aggregate statistics for current Parliament (since July 2024)
aggregateStats.sourceName: openDemocracy / Unlock Democracy analysis of Register
aggregateStats.methodologyNote: Compiled from investigative journalism analysis of the full register. Cross-referenced with official Parliament publications.

giftCategories.notes: Paid by foreign governments, charities, and donors.
Football clubs, FA Premier League, cricket.
Includes 9 MPs receiving Taylor Swift Eras Tour tickets.

genderBreakdown.notes: 74% of highest-earning MPs are male. All top 15 earners in 2024 were male.

concentrationStats.description: In 2021-22, the top 24 MPs (9% of those with outside income) earned 71% of all declared outside income.

contextSentences.headline: 236 of 650 MPs (36%) have declared external income since the July 2024 election, working a combined 32,000 hours in second jobs.
contextSentences.earnings: The five highest-earning MPs declared a combined £2.05m in outside income in less than a year — led by Nigel Farage (£873k) and Geoffrey Cox (£686k).
contextSentences.gifts: 318 MPs declared £1.29m in gifts and hospitality, with overseas trips accounting for 63% of the total value.
contextSentences.expenses: Total MP business costs reached an estimated £157m in 2023-24, up 33% in real terms since 2019-20.
contextSentences.concentration: In 2021-22, the top 24 earners — just 9% of MPs with outside income — took home 71% of all declared earnings.

q1_2026.note: Items declared in register publications between January and March 2026. Overseas travel includes flights, accommodation, and subsistence.
